_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Palm Beach County FL House incumbents (HD 88-92). All confirmed running for 2026 re-election. Primary-source roll-call PDFs + endorsements; cross-party deviations flagged (Gossett-Seidman NO on 6-week ban; Tendrich/Long police-union-backed Democrats).

profile.background: FL House HD-88 (West Palm Beach) since 2022 special election; founder/CEO of Suits for Seniors mentoring nonprofit; former Executive Director of the West Palm Beach Police Athletic League. Running for re-election 2026 (D primary Aug 18, 2026).

jervonte-edmonds@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Pro-choice: voted NAY on SB 300 (2023 six-week ban) and NAY on H 289 (2026 fetal personhood); sponsored a reproductive-care guarantee bill; endorsed by FL Planned Parenthood PAC (2022). Gun-control sponsor (filed 'Responsible Firearm Safety Awareness Month' bill, 2025). Affirmative pro-police community work via West Palm Beach Police Athletic League directorship. Listed Christian on official FL House bio. No cross-party deviation found. Topics with no documented position (marriage, education curricula, religious liberty, economy, elections, federalism, immigration) left null, not inferred.

0.note: Voted NAY on H 289 fetal-personhood bill (Jan 2026); does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (2023 six-week ban); sponsored a bill guaranteeing reproductive/abortion access.

4.note: Endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood PAC (2022 general) — abortion-industry PAC backing.

0.note: Former Executive Director of the West Palm Beach Police Athletic League — affirmative police-partnership program work.
Sponsored gun-control measures; opposite of permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Filed gun-safety legislation; does not oppose gun regulation.

profile.background: FL House HD-89 (Greenacres / Lake Worth Beach, Palm Beach County) since Nov 2024; founder of the Eat Better Live Better nutrition nonprofit (2016). Running unopposed for 2026 re-election.

debra-tendrich@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Pro-choice: publicly backed Amendment 4 (2024), ran on abortion protections, endorsed by Ruth's List (FL pro-choice PAC). Wants income caps on school vouchers and to force voucher-receiving private schools to accept those students; endorsed by Florida Education Association. Gun control: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate'. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: a Democrat broadly endorsed by police/firefighter unions (FL PBA, PBC PBA, FL FOP) and co-sponsor of LEO-benefits bill HB 751 — scored as affirmative law-enforcement support. Jewish; sponsored Holocaust Remembrance Day (HB 251, 2025). Topics with no documented position left null.

0.note: Backed Amendment 4 (2024); ran on abortion protections — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice; no support for abortion restriction let alone abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Ruth's List (FL pro-choice/abortion-rights PAC).

0.note: Wants to restore income caps on school vouchers and restrict private-school choice; endorsed by Florida Education Association.
Endorsed by FL PBA, PBC PBA, FL FOP; co-sponsored HB 751 (law-enforcement officer benefits). Cross-party: a Democrat with strong police-union backing.
Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate'; opposite of permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Gun-control aligned (Moms Demand); does not oppose red-flag/AWB measures.

profile.background: FL House HD-90 (Delray Beach / coastal Palm Beach County); won Dec 9, 2025 special election to succeed the late Joe Casello. Former Delray Beach City Commissioner/Vice Mayor and PBC Soil & Water supervisor. Running for full term in 2026.

rob-long@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Brand-new member (sworn Dec 9, 2025); thin state voting record. Scored from endorsements: Equality Florida + LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus of Palm Beach County (pro-LGBTQ policy → contrary to biblical-marriage rubric). Endorsed by FL Police Benevolent Association and IAFF firefighters; supports competitive first-responder wages → affirmative law-enforcement support. CROSS-PARTY NOTE: also carries FL Chamber of Commerce + Associated Industries of FL endorsements — a center-business, pro-police Democrat. iVoterGuide rated 'Liberal.' Abortion, education, religious liberty, economy specifics, elections, federalism, immigration, guns left null (no documented position; Moms Demand 'gun sense' unconfirmed at primary source).

0.note: Endorsed by Equality Florida and the LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus; does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: LGBTQ-advocacy-endorsed; does not oppose same-sex marriage.

2.note: Equality Florida (leading trans-rights org) endorsement — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Endorsed by LGBTQ-advocacy groups; supports LGBTQ+ in public policy.

0.note: Endorsed by FL Police Benevolent Association and IAFF firefighters; supports competitive first-responder wages.

profile.background: FL House HD-91 (Boca Raton / Highland Beach, coastal Palm Beach County) since Nov 2022. Running for 2026 re-election (two Democratic challengers filed). A coastal-district Republican with one notable cross-party vote.

peggy-gossett-seidman@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: one of three Palm Beach County Republicans to vote NO on SB 300 (2023 six-week abortion ban) — 'It's just not the right vote.' BUT voted YES on H 289 (2026 fetal personhood) and is endorsed by Florida Family Action (pro-life), so she affirms personhood from conception while opposing the six-week ban specifically. Voted YES on HB 543 (2023 permitless carry) and YES on SB 1718 (2023 immigration enforcement, incl. E-Verify) — aligned with GOP on guns and immigration. Jewish. Marriage/gender, education curricula, religious liberty, economy, elections, federalism, public-justice specifics left null (no documented record).

0.note: Voted YES on H 289 fetal-personhood bill (Jan 2026) — affirms personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted NO on SB 300 (2023 six-week ban); did not support abortion restriction/abolition. Cross-party deviation.

4.note: Endorsed by Florida Family Action (pro-life); no abortion-industry PAC funding documented.

3.note: Voted YES on SB 1718 (2023), which mandated/expanded E-Verify for employment.

0.note: Voted YES on HB 543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry).

profile.background: FL House HD-92 (south Palm Beach County); served HD-90 2006-2010, returned to the House 2020 (HD-81), HD-92 since 2022. Running for re-election 2026 (D primary Aug 18, 2026). Long, consistent progressive roll-call record.

kelly-skidmore@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-20. Documented progressive on every marquee FL bill (primary-source roll-call PDFs): NAY SB 300 (six-week ban), NAY HB 5 (fifteen-week ban), NAY HB 1557 ('Don't Say Gay'), NAY HB 1 (universal vouchers), NAY HB 543 (permitless carry), NAY SB 1718 (immigration/E-Verify). 'Abortion is health care.' Endorsed by EMILY's List. Sponsored expansion of FL's red-flag law (family-petition). Historically supported ending FL's gay-adoption ban (2010). No cross-party deviation. Religion not documented. Religious liberty, economy, elections, federalism, public-justice left null (no record recovered this pass).

0.note: 'Abortion is health care'; voted against abortion limits — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted NAY on SB 300 (six-week ban) and HB 5 (fifteen-week ban).

4.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 (Parental Rights in Education / 'Don't Say Gay') — opposed restricting LGBTQ instruction in schools.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 1 (2023 universal school-choice vouchers).

2.note: Voted NAY on HB 1557 ('Don't Say Gay'); did not support limiting SOGI curricula in K-12.

4.note: Historically campaigned to end FL's gay-adoption ban (2010) — supports same-sex-couple adoption.

3.note: Voted NAY on SB 1718 (2023), which mandated E-Verify — opposed the E-Verify mandate.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB 543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry).

1.note: Sponsored expansion of FL's red-flag law (family-member petition) — supports, not opposes, red-flag laws.
